title: White House Targets July 4 for Clarity Act Passage (Crypto Market Structure Bill)
summary: White House digital assets adviser Patrick Witt announced a July 4 target date for passage of the Clarity Act, a crypto market structure bill. Senate Banking Committee hearing expected this month.

title: CFTC Chair Pushes New Rules to Protect Crypto Software Developers
summary: CFTC Chair Michael Selig announced plans for formal rulemaking to clarify broker registration for non-custodial crypto software developers. Rules aim to protect developers from regulatory uncertainty.

title: SEC Hints at New Regulatory Framework for Crypto Trading
summary: SEC signals forthcoming rules of play for crypto trading, indicating a new regulatory framework for digital asset exchanges and trading venues is being developed.

title: US Government Planning Federal Law to Regulate AI Model Releases
summary: Reports indicate the US government is planning federal legislation requiring AI companies (Google, OpenAI, Anthropic) to obtain government approval before releasing AI models. The law would establish pre-release review requirements for frontier AI systems.

title: US Senate Banking Committee to Consider Clarity Act Crypto Bill in Week of May 12
summary: Reuters reports the US Senate Banking Committee is set to consider the Clarity Act crypto market structure bill next week with a committee vote expected around May 14. This follows the White House May 7 announcement targeting July 4 for full passage.

title: Pipeline Safety: Meeting of the Gas Pipeline Advisory Committee
summary: This notice announces a public meeting of the Technical Pipeline Safety Standards Committee, also known as the Gas Pipeline Advisory Committee (GPAC), to discuss the notice of proposed rulemaking (NPRM), titled "Safety of Gas Distribution Pipelines and Other Pipeline Safety Initiatives."

title: Animal Generic Drug User Fee Act; Stakeholder Consultation Meetings on the Animal Generic Drug User Fee Act Reauthorization; Request for Notification of Stakeholder Intent To Participate
summary: The Food and Drug Administration (FDA, the Agency, or we) is issuing this notice to request that public stakeholders notify FDA of their intent to participate in periodic consultation meetings on reauthorization of the Animal Generic Drug User Fee Act (AGDUFA). The statutory authority for AGDUFA expires September 30, 2028. The Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act) requires that FDA consult with a range of stakeholders--including patient and consumer advocacy groups, veterinary prof

title: Animal Drug User Fee Act; Stakeholder Consultation Meetings on the Animal Drug User Fee Act Reauthorization; Request for Notification of Stakeholder Intent To Participate
summary: The Food and Drug Administration (FDA, the Agency, or we) is issuing this notice to request that public stakeholders notify FDA of their intent to participate in periodic consultation meetings on reauthorization of the Animal Drug User Fee Act (ADUFA). The statutory authority for ADUFA expires September 30, 2028. The Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FD&C Act) requires that FDA consult with a range of stakeholders--including patient and consumer advocacy groups, veterinary professionals,

title: Security Zone; Ohio River, Cincinnati, OH
summary: The Coast Guard is establishing a temporary security zone for all navigable waters of the Ohio River, extending the entire width of the river, between mile marker (MM) 461 to MM 473. This security zone is needed to provide waterside security and protection of persons under the protection of the United States Secret Service during a visit to Cincinnati, OH. During the enforcement period, entry into, transiting, or anchoring in the security zone is prohibited unless specifically authorized by the

title: Safety Zone; Bancroft Canal, Cameron Parish, LA
summary: The Coast Guard is establishing a temporary safety zone for navigable waters on the Bancroft Canal between points 30[deg]01'12.67" N, 093[deg]37'23.07" W, and 30[deg]01'12.03" N, 093[deg]36'18.60" W. The safety zone is needed to protect personnel, vessels, and the marine environment from potential hazards created by the active response and cleanup of an oil spill on the waterway. Entry of vessels or persons into this zone is prohibited unless specifically auth

title: Self-Regulatory Organizations; BOX Exchange LLC; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of a Proposed Rule Change To Establish Fees for Industry Members Related to Reasonably Budgeted CAT Costs of the National Market System Plan Governing the Consolidated Audit Trail for the Period From May 1, 2026 Through December 31, 2026

Self-Regulatory Organizations; NYSE National, Inc.; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of Proposed Rule Change To Amend the NYSE National Schedule of Fees and Rebates To Establish Fees for Industry Members Related to Reasonably Budgeted CAT Costs of the National Market System Plan Governing the Consolidated Audit Trail for May 1, 2026 Through December 31, 2026
Self-Regulatory Organizations; 24X National Exchange LLC; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of Proposed Rule Change To Amend the Transaction Fees Applicable to Members of the Exchange

Self-Regulatory Organizations; NYSE American LLC; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of Proposed Rule Change To Amend the NYSE American Equities Price List and the NYSE American Options Fee Schedule To Establish Fees for Industry Members Related To Reasonably Budgeted CAT Costs of the National Market System Plan Governing the Consolidated Audit Trail for May 1, 2026 Through December 31, 2026

Self-Regulatory Organizations; New York Stock Exchange LLC; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of Proposed Rule Change To Amend the NYSE Price List To Establish Fees for Industry Members Related to Reasonably Budgeted CAT Costs of the National Market System Plan Governing the Consolidated Audit Trail for May 1, 2026 Through December 31, 2026

Self-Regulatory Organizations; NYSE Arca, Inc.; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of Proposed Rule Change To Amend the NYSE Arca Equities Fees and Charges and the NYSE Arca Options Fees and Charges To Establish Fees for Industry Members Related to Reasonably Budgeted CAT Costs of the National Market System Plan Governing the Consolidated Audit Trail for May 1, 2026 Through December 31, 2026

Self-Regulatory Organizations; MEMX LLC; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of a Proposed Rule Change To Amend Rule 2.8, Voluntary Termination of Rights as a Member
Self-Regulatory Organizations; MX2 LLC; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of a Proposed Rule Change To Amend Rule 2.8, Voluntary Termination of Rights as a Member

Self-Regulatory Organizations; Cboe C2 Exchange, Inc.; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of a Proposed Rule Change To Amend Its Rule Regarding Trading Permit Holders and Associated Persons of a Trading Permit Holder Who Are or Become Subject to a Statutory Disqualification

Self-Regulatory Organizations; Cboe BZX Exchange, Inc.; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of a Proposed Rule Change To Amend Its Rule Regarding Members and Associated Persons of Members Who Are or Become Subject to a Statutory Disqualification

Self-Regulatory Organizations; Cboe EDGX Exchange, Inc.; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of a Proposed Rule Change To Amend Its Rule Regarding Members and Associated Persons of Members Who Are or Become Subject to a Statutory Disqualification

Self-Regulatory Organizations; Cboe EDGA Exchange, Inc.; Notice of Filing and Immediate Effectiveness of a Proposed Rule Change To Amend Its Rule Regarding Members and Associated Persons of Members Who Are or Become Subject to a Statutory Disqualification

Certain Photodynamic Therapy Systems, Components Thereof, and Pharmaceutical Products Used in Combination With the Same; Notice of the Commission's Final Determination Finding a Violation of Section 337; Issuance of a Limited Exclusion Order and Cease and Desist Orders; Termination of the Investigation
summary: Notice is hereby given that the U.S. International Trade Commission ("Commission") has found a violation of section 337 in the above-captioned investigation. The Commission has determined to issue a limited exclusion order ("LEO") prohibiting the unlicensed entry of infringing oil vaporizing devices, components thereof, and products containing the same that are manufactured by or on behalf of, or imported by or on behalf of, the respondents and cease and desist orders ("

title: Risk Management and Financial Assurance for OCS Lease and Grant Obligations; Extension of Public Comment Period
summary: The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) is extending the public comment period on our notice of proposed rulemaking (NPRM) titled "Risk Management and Financial Assurance for OCS Lease and Grant Obligations" by seven days. Comments previously submitted need not be resubmitted and will be fully considered.

title: Formations of, Acquisitions by, and Mergers of Bank Holding Companies
Revising Firearms Transaction Record, “Form 4473”
summary: The Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives ("ATF") is proposing to amend Department of Justice ("Department") regulations governing ATF Form 5300.9, Firearms Transaction Record, ("Form 4473"). Specifically, ATF proposes streamlining identity and residence verification requirements and documents; doubling the performance timeframe for transactions under Form 4473 following a National Instant Criminal Background Check System ("NICS") check; per

title: Definition of Hazardous Waste Applicable to Corrective Action for Releases From Solid Waste Management Units; Withdrawal
summary: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is withdrawing its proposed rule entitled "Definition of Hazardous Waste Applicable to Corrective Action for Releases From Solid Waste Management Units." The Agency issued the proposed rule to: amend the regulatory definition of hazardous waste applicable to corrective action to address releases from solid waste management units at hazardous waste treatment, storage, and disposal facilities permitted under the Resource Conservation and Rec

title: Amendment of Class E Airspace Over Honesdale, PA
summary: This action amends Class E airspace over Honesdale, PA. The portion of the Class E airspace associated with the Honesdale Sports Complex Heliport is being removed due to the heliport being abandoned and associated instrument approach procedures being canceled.

title: Special Local Regulations; Marine Events Within the USCG East District
summary: The Coast Guard is amending a special local regulation for certain waters of the Patapsco River, in Baltimore, MD by adding a new period during which this regulation would be subject to enforcement. This action is necessary to provide for the safety of life on these navigable waters during the 3rd and 4th weeks of June, during Fleet Week events. This rule prohibits persons and vessels from entering the regulated area during this enforcement period unless authorized by the Captain of the Port Mar

title: Statement on the end of transitional periods under MiCA
summary: ESMA warns that the MiCA transitional period expires 1 July 2026. Entities providing crypto-asset services without a MiCA licence will be in breach of EU law and must cease offering such services.

title: Guidelines on Endorsement under Article 11 of the ESG Rating Regulation
summary: ESMA consulting on endorsement guidelines for ESG rating providers under the new ESG Rating Regulation. Deadline 29 May 2026.

title: EBA consultation on simplification of credit risk framework
summary: EBA consulting on major simplification of the credit risk framework, including simplification of supervisory reporting. Discussion period closes 10 May 2026.

title: EU Digital Omnibus on AI – Political Deal Reached on AI Act Amendments
summary: Political deal reached on the EU Digital Omnibus on AI which will amend and consolidate the existing AI Act framework. The deal introduces changes to regulatory requirements for high-risk systems, foundation models, and compliance obligations.

title: Irish DPC Opens Inquiry Into SHEIN Over EU Data Transfers to China
summary: The Irish Data Protection Commission opened an inquiry into SHEIN regarding EU personal data transfers to China, potentially violating GDPR cross-border data transfer requirements. Investigation focuses on adequacy and appropriate safeguards.

title: ECB Executive Escrivá Calls for Financial Infrastructure Review Due to AI Risks
summary: ECB Executive Board member Escrivá called for a comprehensive review of financial infrastructure to address risks posed by AI, including operational resilience, model risk management, and systemic risk considerations.

title: EU Countries and Lawmakers Fail to Reach Deal on Watered-Down AI Omnibus Rules
summary: EU member states and European Parliament lawmakers failed to reach an agreement on the AI Omnibus package, which aimed to simplify and streamline the EU AI Act for businesses. The failure signals continued regulatory uncertainty for AI companies operating in the EU.

title: Dutch Data Protection Authority Issues €100M GDPR Fine Against Yango for Data Flows to Russia
summary: The Dutch Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens issued a €100 million GDPR fine against Yango (Yandex subsidiary) for unlawful data transfers to Russia. The fine is one of the largest GDPR enforcement actions in 2026, highlighting ongoing scrutiny of cross-border data transfers outside the EU.

title: EU Countries and Lawmakers Clinch Provisional Deal on Watered-Down AI Omnibus Rules
summary: UPDATE: After initially failing to reach agreement, EU member states and European Parliament lawmakers have now clinched a provisional deal on amended AI Act rules, clarifying overlaps with machinery regulation and simplifying compliance requirements. The deal represents a significant scaling-back from the original AI Act provisions.

title: ESMA Warns: MiCA Transitional Period Expires 1 July 2026 — Unlicensed Crypto Firms Must Cease EU Services
summary: ESMA issued a statement reminding market participants that the MiCA transitional period expires 1 July 2026. After that date, any entity providing crypto-asset services to EU clients without a MiCA licence will be in breach of EU law and must cease offering such services. ESMA expects unauthorised firms to have wind-down plans in place and ensure orderly exit without harm to clients.

title: EBA Amends Guidelines on the Definition of Default — CRR 3 Alignment
summary: Final Report amending Guidelines on the application of the definition of default. Introduces targeted amendments for non-recourse factoring and confirms the 1% NPV threshold in debt restructuring remains appropriate for prudential default recognition.

title: EU Approves Sanctions on Israeli Settlers and Hamas — Crypto Compliance Stakes Rise
summary: The European Union approved new sanctions targeting Israeli settlers and Hamas figures, raising compliance obligations for crypto-asset service providers operating in the EU. Regulated firms must ensure enhanced screening and transaction monitoring under expanded sanctions lists.

title: ECB President Lagarde Speech: Stablecoins and the Future of Money — Separating Functions from Instruments
summary: ECB President Christine Lagarde delivered a speech at the Bank of Spain LatAm Economic Forum addressing stablecoins' rapid growth from $10B to $300B+ in six years. She emphasized that MiCA brought stablecoins within the regulatory perimeter in 2024, noting concerns about financial stability risks particularly in Latin America and Africa. Lagarde argued the question is not whether stablecoins should exist but whether jurisdictions can afford to be without them, and called for separating monetary policy functions from payment instruments.

title: MiCA Transitional Period Statement — Expiry 1 July 2026
summary: ESMA issued a statement on the end of MiCA transitional periods. The transitional period will officially expire across the EU on 1 July 2026. After that date, entities providing crypto-asset services without a MiCA licence will be in breach of EU law.

title: ECB Updates Compendium of Good Practices for Climate and Nature-Related Risk Management
summary: The ECB released its updated compendium of good practices for climate and nature-related risk management and stress testing. ECB Executive Board Member Frank Elderson warned that physical and nature-related risks are very likely being underestimated by banks.

title: Apple, Google Warn EU AI Access Rules Threaten Privacy & Security - Global Banking & Finance Review
summary: Apple, Google Warn EU AI Access Rules Threaten Privacy & Security - Global Banking & Finance Review

title: ESMA issues guidance on effective use of resolution tools in CCP crisis planning
summary: ESMA issued guidance on the effective use of resolution tools in central counterparty (CCP) crisis planning, providing direction on how CCPs should prepare for and manage resolution scenarios to ensure financial stability.

title: EU Fails to Reach Agreement on AI Act Implementing Rules
summary: EU countries and lawmakers failed to reach agreement on implementing rules for the EU AI Act, signaling ongoing friction in translating the landmark legislation into enforceable standards. May delay enforcement timelines for high-risk AI systems.

title: Polish Lawmakers Adopt Crypto Regulation Bill Under EU MiCA Framework
summary: Polish parliament adopted a crypto regulation bill implementing the EU MiCA framework, prompted by a multi-million dollar fraud scandal. The bill establishes licensing requirements for crypto service providers and consumer protection measures aligned with pan-European standards.

title: Poland Passes MiCA Implementation Bill to Regulate Crypto Assets
summary: Polish lawmakers approved a comprehensive crypto regulation bill implementing the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework. The bill was passed amid a $96M fraud probe involving Zondacrypto exchange and represents the final major EU member state adoption ahead of the MiCA compliance deadline. President had previously vetoed two earlier versions.

title: EU Agrees Digital Omnibus Deal to Simplify AI Rules
summary: EU institutions reached a provisional agreement on the Digital Omnibus package that simplifies certain aspects of the AI Act implementation. The deal would delay some high-risk AI rule requirements and streamline compliance obligations for businesses, while maintaining core safety and transparency provisions of the original AI Act.

title: European Parliament Finalises Securitisation Regulation and CRR Reform Proposals
summary: The European Parliament finalised reform proposals for the Securitisation Regulation and Capital Requirements Regulation (CRR) ahead of trilogue negotiations. The reforms aim to reduce regulatory burden for banks while maintaining financial stability, and will reshape how European banks originate and hold securitised assets.

title: Poland Passes Crypto Regulation Bill to Align with EU MiCA Framework
summary: Polish lawmakers adopted a comprehensive crypto regulation bill after repeated presidential vetoes, aligning national law with the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework. The legislation comes amid a multi-million dollar fraud probe and establishes licensing requirements for crypto service providers operating in Poland.

title: Poland Passes MiCA Crypto Bill as 96 Million Dollar Zondacrypto Fraud Probe Deepens
summary: Polish lawmakers adopted a new crypto regulation implementing MiCA framework, making Poland one of the last EU member states to transpose the regulation. The bill comes amid a deepening political divide over a 96 million dollar fraud probe involving Zondacrypto exchange. The president had previously vetoed two earlier versions.

title: EU Reaches Provisional Agreement on Digital Omnibus on AI — AI Act Amendments & Deadline Extensions
summary: On May 7, 2026, the European Parliament and Council reached a provisional agreement on the Digital Omnibus on AI, reducing rule overlap, introducing new prohibitions, and extending deadlines for high-risk AI systems (Annex III to Dec 2027; Annex IV to Aug 2028). New transparency obligations enter force Aug 2026.

title: EU Digital Fairness Act: Consumer Advocates and Industry Groups React to Draft Legislation
summary: The European Commission is currently drafting the Digital Fairness Act, a major legislative initiative aimed at adapting consumer protection rules to the digital age. Consumer advocates see it as an important step forward, while business representatives warn it could create excessive bureaucracy. The legislation is expected to address dark patterns, exploitative personalization, and other digital consumer protection issues.

title: ESMA Issues Guidance on Resolution Tools in CCP Crisis Planning
summary: ESMA has published a resolution briefing for Central Counterparties (CCPs), providing practical guidance to National Resolution Authorities on how to operationalise the write-down and conversion of instruments tool (WDCI).

title: ESMA Consults on ESG Ratings Regulation Endorsement Guidelines
summary: ESMA is consulting on guidelines on endorsement under the ESG Ratings Regulation, establishing how ESG rating providers operating in the EU should be endorsed.

title: Apple Sides With Google In Fighting EU's AI Measures
summary: Apple has aligned with Google in opposing the EU's proposed AI regulatory measures under the AI Act, arguing that the requirements would stifle innovation and create competitive disadvantages for non-EU tech companies. The companies are pushing back against transparency and risk assessment requirements for general-purpose AI models.

title: ESMA consults on revised guidelines to support smoother allocations and confirmations under T+1
summary:
ESMA consults on revised guidelines to support smoother allocations and confirmations under T+1
  
26 May 2026


      
              Post Trading
          
  
            The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA), the EU’s financial markets regulator and supervisor, has launched a consultation on the updated guidelines on standardised procedures and messaging protocols. This review is part of ESMA’s work to support market participants in preparing for the transition to a T+1 settlem

title: Competition Act 1998 Investigations - PayPal / Mastercard / Visa
summary: FCA confirms it is investigating Mastercard, PayPal, and Visa under Chapter I and Chapter II of the Competition Act 1998 for suspected anti-competitive conduct linked to the funding and usage of PayPal digital wallet. No findings have been made yet.

title: CP26/13: Cryptoasset Perimeter Guidance (Consultation)
summary: FCA consultation on proposed perimeter guidance for UK crypto regime under FSMA 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026. Covers stablecoins, cryptoasset safeguarding, trading platforms, dealing, arranging. Consultation closes 3 June 2026.

title: FCA Update on Legal Challenges to Motor Finance Compensation Scheme
summary: FCA issued an update statement for firms and consumers regarding legal challenges to the motor finance compensation scheme. This follows ongoing judicial review proceedings affecting motor finance commission disclosures. The update provides guidance to firms on compliance expectations during the legal challenge period.

title: FCA EquiteFarms warning and Edgebitpro new warning
summary: FCA updated warning on EquiteFarms and issued new warning on Edgebitpro (8 May 2026), flagging potentially unauthorized firms in crypto/investment space.

title: FCA Legal challenges to motor finance compensation scheme - update for firms and consumers
summary: FCA issued update on legal challenges to motor finance compensation scheme, providing guidance for affected firms and consumers.

title: FCA Cryptoasset firms pre-application meetings available from 11 May 2026
summary: FCA announced cryptoasset firms seeking authorization can request pre-application meetings starting 11 May 2026, part of UK crypto regulatory framework rollout.

title: Three arrested in FCA probe into suspected unlawful financial promotions
summary: Three individuals arrested in connection with FCA investigation into suspected unlawful financial promotions.

title: Convicted money launderer sentenced to extra prison time
summary:
A convicted money launderer has been sentenced to an additional 499 daysin prison for failing to fully pay the money owed under a Confiscation Order.

In 2021,RichardFaithfull,now36,wassentenced to5 years and 10 monthsin prisonfor laundering £2.5 million, following a prosecution brought by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA).He was part of a trans-national organised crime group which laundered the proceeds of at least 7 overseas investment frauds.Mr Faithfullis required topay back £529,961,bas

title: Three arrested in FCA investigation into suspected unlawful financial promotions
summary:
Three people have been arrested as part of a crackdown on suspected illegal financial promotions.

Two homes in the Chelmsford and Romford areas were searched, as part of an operation led by the FCA and the Eastern Regional Special Operations Unit (ERSOU), a specialist policing unit that tackles serious and organised crime.Adverts from firms that aren't FCA-regulated can be a warning sign of a scam. If something goes wrong, these firms aren't covered by the rules that protect people's money – me

title: A reform-minded regulator
summary:
Speech by Nikhil Rathi, FCA chief executive, at the Association of Foreign Banks (AFB) luncheon.

When I saw that a boxing ring had been temporarily installed in this room last autumn, I wasn’t quite sure whether it was a warning to us regulators…Or some kind of art installation commenting on the past few years in financial markets.At some points it has felt bruising, to say the least.Some pressures have been sharp and immediate – geopolitical shocks, sudden market events.Others slower but no le

title: FCA charges Shaun Lawrence for unauthorised mortgage broking
summary:
The FCA has charged Shaun Lawrence for operating as a mortgage broker without authorisation.

Mr Lawrence, who also goes by the names Shaun Lawrence-Bright and Shaun Bright, was previously authorised to give mortgage advice.However, in 2008 he had his permissions revoked and was fined. He was also banned from working in a regulated role within financial services.The FCA alleges that Mr Lawrence has breached the Financial Services and Markets Act by continuing to provide mortgage broking services

title: Trust, tradition and the future of mutual growth
summary:
Speech by Sarah Pritchard, FCA deputy chief executive, at the BSA Annual Conference, Edinburgh.

As a history lover, it’s thrilling to be in a city like Edinburgh – called a ‘hot-bed of genius’ during the Scottish Enlightenment.What defined the Enlightenment spirit was the refusal to settle, and a determination to make things better for the future.It’s the kind of approach I’m taking to this moment of regulatory reform.Working with others to solve difficult problems, protecting trust and good ou

title: FCA Board appoints 2 new members to the Regulatory Decisions Committee
summary:
The FCA Board appoints new members to decision-making committee.

The Board of the FCA has appointed Jonathan Peddie and Raymond Cox KC as new members of the FCA’s Regulatory Decisions Committee (RDC).The RDC is responsible for taking certain regulatory decisions on behalf of the FCA relating to contested enforcement action. Committee members bring a broad range of professional experience to support fair, independent and evidence-based decision-making.Alison Potter, the chair of the RDC, said:'I

title: Consultation on the UK Treasury bill market
Correspondence: Open letters between HM Treasury and Bank of England, April 2026
Competition Act 1998 investigations into Mastercard, PayPal and Visa
summary: The FCA is investigating Mastercard, PayPal and Visa under Chapter I in the Competition Act 1998, and Mastercard and Visa under Chapter II, for suspected anti-competitive conduct linked to the funding and usage of PayPal's digital wallet.

title: Bank of England Governor Foresees 'Wrestle' with US on Stablecoin Regulation, Warns of Stability Risks
summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey indicated the UK will face regulatory challenges competing with US stablecoin legislation (GENIUS Act). Bailey cautioned that stablecoin adoption poses stability risks for the UK and that the BoE may pursue tighter stablecoin rules than currently proposed, potentially requiring issuers to hold backing assets in UK-regulated institutions.

title: Commercial Credit Data Sharing and Bank Referral Scheme: Consultation and Call for Evidence
FCA fines and bans Frank Breuer for serious misconduct in pension transfer advice
summary:
The FCA has banned Frank Breuer from working in UK financial services and fined him £755,000 for repeatedly acting without integrity and putting customers at risk for personal financial gain.

Mr Breuer was the joint owner and sole director of Bluesky Wealth Management Limited (Bluesky), which provided advice on investments and pensions. Although authorised to advise on defined benefit (DB) pension transfers, the firm did not have the appropriate professional insurance in place from April 2019.

title: ICO fines South Staffordshire Plc and South Staffordshire Water Plc £963,900 for major data breach
summary: The ICO fined South Staffordshire Plc and South Staffordshire Water Plc £963,900 following a serious cyber attack that resulted in the personal information of 633,887 people being extracted and published on the dark web. Key enforcement action for critical infrastructure data security.

title: MHRA strengthens safety warnings for finasteride and dutasteride
summary: Updated safety advice issued to strengthen warnings about potential psychiatric and sexual dysfunction linked to finasteride and dutasteride, impacting prescribing guidelines and pharmaceutical company obligations.

title: MHRA proposes new pre-market regulatory requirements for medical devices in GB
summary: New pre-market regulatory requirements for medical devices and in vitro diagnostic (IVD) devices entering the GB market have been published for consultation, representing the UK's post-Brexit medical devices regulatory framework independent of EU MDR.

title: FCA bans and fines director £755,000 for advice and insurance failures
summary: UK FCA banned a financial advisor director and imposed a £755,000 fine for failures in advice and insurance recommendations.

title: UK watchdog bans pension advisory director for misconduct
summary: UK FCA banned a pension advisory firm director for misconduct in pension transfer recommendations.

title: Working together against financial crime
summary:
Speech by Nikhil Rathi, FCA chief executive at the FCA's financial crime conference.

A new threat landscapeFinancial crime is changing – fast.It’s more technologically enabled. More organised than ever before. And moving at speed.Which is why the fight against financial crime sits at the heart of our 5-year strategy.But it’s not just the volume that’s changed; it’s who is behind it.Organised criminal groups running professional networks that operate across borders.Take investment fraud.A person

title: FCA Fines and Bans Frank Breuer for Serious Misconduct in Pension Transfer Advice
summary: FCA banned Frank Breuer from UK financial services and fined him £755,000 for acting without integrity. As sole director of Bluesky Wealth Management, Breuer carried out 16+ defined benefit pension transfers while knowingly uninsured, repeatedly misled the FCA, stripped firm assets via dividends and loans, and placed firm into insolvency leaving £214,772 in customer liabilities to the FSCS.

title: Independent report: HM Treasury – Access to Banking Services Review
Government reviews access to face to face banking services
FCA, Bank of England and Treasury joint statement on frontier AI models and cyber resilience
summary: Why frontier AI matters for firmsArtificial intelligence (AI) continues to evolve rapidly. Frontier AI models represent a step-change in capability, with significant implications for cyber security and operational resilience.The cyber capabilities of current frontier AI models are already exceeding what a skilled practitioner could achieve, and at a significantly higher speed, greater scale, and lower cost. These capabilities, if used maliciously, amplify cyber threats to firms’ safety and sound

title: Bank of England May Rethink UK Stablecoin Restrictions as TradFi Pushes Back
summary: Bank of England Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden indicated the BoE is reconsidering two controversial stablecoin proposals: £20,000/£10m individual/business holding caps and 40% unremunerated central bank reserve requirement. The BoE acknowledged it may have been 'overly conservative' in its liquidity stress assumptions. Consultation responses from UK Finance and AFME reveal regulated financial institutions are divided on the scope of restrictions.

title: Singapore Parliament Passes Securities and Futures (Amendment) Bill for Global Listing Board
summary: Parliament passed amendments to the Securities and Futures Act creating a new legislative framework for the Global Listing Board (GLB) — a third equity board on SGX dedicated to dual SGX/Nasdaq listings. Companies can now list on both exchanges simultaneously with reduced paperwork and a single set of offering documents. MAS empowered to harmonize securities laws between Singapore and foreign countries. GLB set to go live mid-2026.

title: MAS Removes Mandatory Financial Advice for Complex Products
summary: MAS finalized changes to streamline distribution safeguards for complex financial products, removing the mandatory pre-purchase financial advice requirement for most retail investors purchasing investment-linked policies. The regulation still applies to vulnerable groups aged 62+. MAS also enhanced Product Highlights Sheet (PHS) requirements.

Japan FSA Tells Real Estate and Crypto Sectors to Tighten AML Checks on Property Deals
summary: Japan's Financial Services Agency has instructed real estate and cryptocurrency sectors to implement enhanced anti-money laundering checks on property transactions. The directive targets the use of crypto assets in real estate purchases as a money laundering vector, requiring stricter KYC and transaction monitoring.

title: Japan's Top Banks Launch Stablecoin Pilot With FSA Backing
summary: Japan's three megabanks (MUFG, SMBC, Mizuho) launched a stablecoin pilot program with FSA regulatory support, marking Japan's first major bank-backed stablecoin initiative.

title: Japan FSA: Working Group on Strengthening Cybersecurity Against AI-Related Threats in Financial Sector
summary: Japan's FSA convened a working group under the Public-Private Coordination Meeting on Strengthening Cybersecurity Measures in the Financial Sector Against AI-Related Threats, addressing emerging cybersecurity risks from AI technology in financial services.

title: Japan FSA Report on Strengthening Third-Party Cybersecurity Risk Management
summary: FSA published research report on strengthening management of third-party cybersecurity risks by financial institutions, providing guidance on vendor risk management and cyber resilience.

title: Japan FSA Shareholding Disclosure Reform - Control-Based Transparency Rules
summary: Japan's FSA shareholding disclosure rules changed May 1, 2026, shifting from capital-based to control-based transparency. Part of a twelve-year arc of governance reform.

title: Japan FSA Crypto Tax Reform - Tax Rate Reduction from 55% to 20%
summary: Japan's FSA plans to cut crypto tax to 20% and classify crypto assets as financial products under FIEA, applying mandatory disclosures and insider-trading rules.
